title: Travel and Directions
description: Get around in Spanish: transport (el tren, el avión, el metro), the station and airport (¿A qué hora sale?, andén, retraso, hacer transbordo), asking and giving directions with commands (sigue todo recto, gira a la derecha, cruza la calle) and booking a hotel room. Includes the false friend realizar (NOT 'to realize').

cards.front: el tren, el avión, el autobús, el metro
cards.back: the train, the plane, the bus, the metro/subway
cards.notes: Core transport vocabulary. Note *el avión* has an accent (plural *los aviones*, no accent). *El autobús* is often shortened to *el bus*. The verb for travelling by these is *ir en*: *ir en tren* = to go by train.

cards.front: el billete / el boleto, la parada
cards.notes: In Spain *el billete*; in much of Latin America *el boleto*. *La parada* is a bus/metro stop; *¿Dónde está la parada del autobús?* = Where is the bus stop? A round-trip ticket is *un billete de ida y vuelta*.
Use **estar** for the location of a place: *La estación **está** cerca* = The station is nearby. English speakers often reach for *es*, but a physical location takes *estar*.

cards.front: ¿A qué hora sale / llega el tren?
cards.back: What time does the train leave / arrive?
cards.notes: *salir* = to leave/depart, *llegar* = to arrive. These are the two questions you need at any station: *¿A qué hora sale?* and *¿A qué hora llega?* The reply: *Sale a las ocho* = It leaves at eight.

cards.front: el andén, la puerta de embarque, el retraso
cards.back: the platform, the boarding gate, the delay
cards.notes: *El andén* = train platform; *la puerta de embarque* = airport boarding gate; *el retraso* = delay. *El tren lleva retraso* = The train is delayed (literally 'carries a delay').
*Hacer transbordo* = to switch trains or metro lines on a journey. *Tienes que hacer transbordo en la próxima estación* = You have to change at the next station. It uses the verb *hacer*.

cards.back: How do I get to...? / Where is...?
cards.notes: Two essential questions. *¿Cómo llego a la estación?* = How do I get to the station? *¿Dónde está el museo?* = Where is the museum? Location always uses **está** (from *estar*), not *es*.

cards.front: sigue todo recto, gira a la derecha / izquierda, cruza la calle
cards.back: go straight on, turn right / left, cross the street
cards.notes: These are **informal (tú) commands**: *seguir* → **sigue**, *girar* → **gira**, *cruzar* → **cruza**. Spanish uses the command form directly for directions; there is no 'you should' wrapper needed.

cards.front: a la vuelta de la esquina, enfrente de, al lado de
cards.back: around the corner, opposite / across from, next to
cards.notes: Position phrases: *a la vuelta de la esquina* = around the corner, *enfrente de* = opposite, *al lado de* = next to. *El banco está al lado del hotel* = The bank is next to the hotel (de + el → del).

cards.back: to book a double / single room
cards.notes: *Reservar* = to book/reserve; *una habitación doble* = a double room, *individual* = single. *¿Tiene una habitación libre?* = Do you have a room available? (*libre* = free/available).

cards.back: realizar = to carry out / accomplish; 'to realize (become aware)' = darse cuenta de
cards.notes: **False friend!** *realizar* does NOT mean 'to realize'. It means to carry out or accomplish: *realizar un viaje* = to make/carry out a trip. To **realize** = become aware = **darse cuenta de**: *Me di cuenta de que perdí el tren* = I realized I missed the train. Never say *Realicé que...* for 'I realized that...'.

cards.back: Do you have a room available?
cards.notes: Polite hotel question using *usted* (*tiene*). *libre* = free/available. The reply might be *Sí, ¿para cuántas noches?* = Yes, for how many nights? Compare *individual* (single) and *doble* (double).

steps.title: Getting around: transport and the station
steps.body:
# Travel and Directions

This lesson gives you the language to move through a Spanish-speaking city or country.

**Transport:**

| Spanish | English |
|---|---|
| el tren | the train |
| el avión | the plane |
| el autobús (el bus) | the bus |
| el metro | the metro / subway |
| el billete / el boleto | the ticket |
| la parada | the (bus) stop |

Use *ir en* + transport: *Voy en metro* = I'm going by metro.

**At the station / airport**, two questions do most of the work:

> *¿A qué hora **sale** el tren?* — What time does the train leave?
> *¿A qué hora **llega** el avión?* — What time does the plane arrive?

Useful nouns: **el andén** (platform), **la puerta de embarque** (boarding gate), **el retraso** (delay), and **hacer transbordo** (to change trains/lines).

> ⚠️ **Location uses *estar*.** *La estación **está** cerca.* English speakers often want *es*, but the physical location of a place always takes **estar**: *El aeropuerto está lejos.*

steps.title: Asking for and giving directions
steps.body:
## Directions

**Asking:**

- *¿Cómo llego a la plaza?* — How do I get to the square?
- *¿Dónde está el banco?* — Where is the bank?

**Giving directions** — Spanish uses the **informal (tú) command** form directly. There is no need for 'you should':

| Verb | Command (tú) | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| seguir | **sigue** todo recto | go straight on |
| girar | **gira** a la derecha | turn right |
| girar | **gira** a la izquierda | turn left |
| cruzar | **cruza** la calle | cross the street |

**Position phrases:**

- *está **a la vuelta de la esquina*** — it's around the corner
- *está **enfrente de** la iglesia* — it's opposite the church
- *está **al lado del** hotel* — it's next to the hotel

Remember *de + el → del*: *al lado **del** banco*.

steps.title: The hotel — and a dangerous false friend
steps.body:
## At the hotel

- *¿**Tiene una habitación libre**?* — Do you have a room available?
- *Quiero **reservar una habitación doble**.* — I want to book a double room.
- *una habitación **individual*** — a single room

## False friend: realizar ≠ to realize

This one trips up almost every English speaker.

> **realizar** does **NOT** mean 'to realize'.

*realizar* = **to carry out, to accomplish, to make (happen)**:
- *Quiero **realizar** un viaje a Perú.* = I want to make a trip to Peru.

To **realize** (= to become aware) = **darse cuenta de**:
- *Me **di cuenta de** que perdí el tren.* = I realized I missed the train.

✗ *Realicé que perdí el tren.* — wrong, this does not mean 'I realized'.
✓ *Me di cuenta de que perdí el tren.*

exercise.prompt: Match each Spanish word with its English meaning.

exercise.hint: Location of a place uses está (from estar), not es.

exercise.prompt: Translate: *I realized that I missed the train.* (become aware — NOT realizar!)
accept: Me di cuenta de que perdí el tren.
me di cuenta de que perdi el tren
Me di cuenta de que perdí el tren
Me di cuenta que perdí el tren.
exercise.hint: 'to realize / become aware' = darse cuenta de. Do NOT use realizar.
distractors: Me realicé que perdí el tren.

exercise.prompt: Fill in the verb for 'leaves / departs'. Ask what time the train leaves.
exercise.hint: salir = to leave/depart. The él/ella form is sale.

exercise.prompt: Arrange the words: *Turn right and cross the street.*
exercise.hint: Use tú commands: girar → gira, cruzar → cruza.

exercise.prompt: On the map, the bank is straight ahead at the end of the street. Which direction tells you to 'go straight on'?
Translate: *I want to book a double room.*
exercise.hint: reservar = to book; una habitación doble = a double room.

exercise.prompt: Fill in the noun: you have to 'transfer / change' at the next station.
exercise.sentence: Tienes que hacer ___ en la próxima estación.
exercise.hint: hacer ___ = to change trains/lines. The noun is transbordo.

exercise.prompt: Match each Spanish position phrase with its English meaning.

pairs.left: a la vuelta de la esquina

exercise.prompt: Arrange the words: *The hotel is around the corner.*
exercise.hint: Location uses está; 'around the corner' = a la vuelta de la esquina.

exercise.prompt: Translate (formal/usted): *Do you have a room available?*
exercise.hint: usted form of tener = tiene; 'available' = libre.

exercise.prompt: Translate: *I want to make a trip to Peru.* (here 'make/carry out' = realizar)
accept: Quiero realizar un viaje a Perú.
quiero realizar un viaje a peru
Quiero realizar un viaje a Perú
Quiero hacer un viaje a Perú.
exercise.hint: realizar = to carry out / make (happen). realizar un viaje = to make a trip.
distractors: Quiero darme cuenta de un viaje a Perú.
Quiero realizar que un viaje a Perú.
Me doy cuenta de un viaje a Perú.
